figures.caption: Fig. 3: Qualitative comparison on TMP 640k. Selected examples of the pathfinding results. Expanded nodes are shown in green, and the resulting path is shown in red. PPMs shown only for methods that produce them.

Fig. 1: One prior, flexible path adaptation. A task-agnostic soft path mask (left) is fine-tuned via PSO to yield shortest-path, obstacle-avoidance, and waypointconstrained routes (via WP) without training from scratch.
Table 1: Shortest-path and zero-shot generalization results. All models trained on TMP 640k. PT: Stage 1 only, FT: Stage 2 only, PT+FT: full pipeline.
Table 3: Obstacle-clearance evaluation on TMP 640k. PT and PT+FT share the same Stage 1 pretrained prior. Full Avoidance is computed on feasible instances for each dmin.
Table 2: Ablation studies on TMP 640k. Each row modifies one component relative to the Stage 1 baseline (Weighted BCE, RGB, UNet+Att).

Fig. 2: Overview of FlexPath. Stage 1 (top): Given an input map X, a neural network fθ predicts a raw path field P (logits), learning a task-agnostic feasibility prior via weighted binary cross-entropy against rasterized planner demonstrations Y. Stage 2 (bottom): The prior is adapted via differentiable Path Shape Objectives (PSOs) encoding task-specific preferences (e.g., shortest-path cost or obstacle clearance). Stage 2 is weakly supervised: Y provides only a scalar cost budget (not spatial targets), while path shape is determined by PSO functionals on the soft path mask C.
